page: Vultr voc-c-4c-8gb-75s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server powered by an AMD EPYC processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core under a shared CPU allocation model. The system is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided by a local 75 GB NVMe SSD, and the configuration includes 1 complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present on this instance.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the top tier (top 10%). Multi-core stress-ng performance and PassMark CPU scores fall within the average range (middle 50%). Memory bandwidth write performance for large blocks is strong (top 25%), while read bandwidth remains average. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks perform strongly, though multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is weak (bottom 25%). Database operations and static web serving throughput also register in the lower performance tiers.

The shared CPU allocation introduces potential resource contention, which is reflected in the lower multi-threaded decompression and database benchmark results. However, the server offers a cost-effective entry point for compute-focused workloads that benefit from strong single-core execution and fast NVMe storage without requiring dedicated CPU cores. It is suitable for lightweight single-threaded processing, basic web serving, development environments, and small-scale model inference tasks.
description: Vultr voc-c-4c-8gb-75s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 4 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC x86_64 processor with a shared CPU allocation. The hardware configuration includes a 75 GB local NVMe SSD and lacks a dedicated GPU. Benchmark data reveals top-tier single-core CPU performance, alongside strong memory write bandwidth and single-threaded compression speeds. However, multi-threaded decompression, database operations, and static web serving throughput fall into lower performance tiers. This resource profile offers a cost-effective option for workloads prioritizing single-core execution over sustained multi-threaded performance. It is best suited for lightweight compute tasks, software development, and small-scale model inference, while being less optimal for heavy database workloads.
og_description: Vultr voc-c-4c-8gb-75s-amd is an AMD EPYC-powered server with 4 shared vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and a 75 GB NVMe SSD, offering top-tier single-core performance for lightweight compute workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-c-4c-8gb-75s-amd features 4 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 75 GB NVMe storage, delivering strong single-core CPU performance.
tagline: A shared-core AMD EPYC compute instance featuring strong single-core performance and fast NVMe storage for lightweight processing workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC x86_64 processor with 4 shared vCPUs and 2 physical cores
8.0 GB system memory and 75 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong memory write bandwidth
Lower performance in multi-threaded decompression and database operations
Well-suited for lightweight single-threaded tasks and development environments
